raw_consumer_statement:
I had a tour with XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX for an apartment building. This was virtual. They said I needed to pay a {$75.00} application fee for the apartment was necessary. They said they had a promotion where the {$400.00} move-in fee gets waived if I submit my application within XXXX XXXX. So I submitted it and they said they needed the {$470.00} to hold my place and it would go towards rent. They never said it would not be refunded if I decided to cancel. They said if I didn't get passed the background screening it would be refunded. This is what XXXX XXXX, the representative I worked with said in person. I went to see the apartment the next day. She in no way clearly indicated that the {$470.00} would not be refundable. 


No where in the disclaimer did it say the {$400.00} was non-refundable. Did not say that in the emails, on the website, or any of the background check documents I signed. Those documents are no longer available to me but only pertained to ensuring that all information I was submitting was accurate. They did not have to do with payments. 

I tried cancelling my application via email and over the phone and they were unwilling to issue a refund. I also tried to dispute this with Citi Advantage and they did not ask for any information from me and did not resolve in my favor. 

As shown in the emails, they only disclosed this after I tried to cancel my application. I had read some reviews about elevators getting stuck and amenities not working and was no longer interested in leasing.
